title: Shailaputri Chalisa
intro: Shri Shailaputri Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Shailaputri
description:
|| Shri Shailputri Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Ganesh Girija suvan, mangal karan kripal.
Vighna haran mangal murti, jai jai Girija lal.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Girirajkumari jagat janani.
Sakal srishti palak Bhavani Bhavani.
Jai Shailputri mata mahima apar.
Jo koi tumko dhyavat bhav bhav paar.
Chandrardh mastak virajat subh Ganga.
Tumhi dekhi harshat hiya Shiv sanga.
Vaahan vrishabh rajat chhavi nirali.
Sohat roop matu Shiv ki lalali.
Kahat ashtama mahima amrit vaani.
Mahima aparmpar vidhi na jaani.
Kali kalak paap hatavni harta.
Santan prabhu preeti prabhu Bhavani karta.
Jo koi tuhi dhyavat rudhi raasi Bhavani.
Sakal srishti palak Durga Bhavani.
Gauri Shankar sang virajati suhavani.
Mangal karan Kali mai kehlavani.
Dhyan dharat jo koi nar Bhavani.
Sakal srishti mein hot subani.
Shri Shailputri Chalisa ka paath.
Karat dhyan jas aapni daas.
Vinay Ram Das manu preetam Bhavani.
Tasu dhyan se sakal srishti Bhavani.
meaning:
|| Shri Shailputri Stuti ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Ganesh Girija suvan, mangal karan kripal.
(Shri Ganesh, the son of Girija (Parvati), is the doer of auspiciousness and merciful.)
Vighna haran mangal murti, jai jai Girija lal.
(The remover of obstacles, the image of auspiciousness, victory to the beloved son of Girija.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Girirajkumari jagat janani.
(Victory to the Daughter of the Mountain King, Mother of the World.)
Sakal srishti palak Bhavani Bhavani.
(You are the protector of the entire creation, Bhavani.)
Jai Shailputri mata mahima apar.
(Victory to Mother Shailputri, your glory is infinite.)
Jo koi tumko dhyavat bhav bhav paar.
(Whoever meditates on you crosses the ocean of existence.)
Chandrardh mastak virajat subh Ganga.
(The half-moon adorns your forehead, along with the auspicious Ganga.)
Tumhi dekhi harshat hiya Shiv sanga.
(Seeing you, the heart of Shiva rejoices.)
Vaahan vrishabh rajat chhavi nirali.
(Your vehicle, the Bull (Vrishabh), looks unique in its radiance.)
Sohat roop matu Shiv ki lalali.
(Your form, Mother, along with Shiva, looks beautiful.)
Kahat ashtama mahima amrit vaani.
(The devotee speaks of your glory in nectar-like words.)
Mahima aparmpar vidhi na jaani.
(Your glory is infinite; even Brahma does not know its extent.)
Kali kalak paap hatavni harta.
(You are the remover of the sins of the Kali Yuga.)
Santan prabhu preeti prabhu Bhavani karta.
(You are the Lord Bhavani who loves the saints.)
Jo koi tuhi dhyavat rudhi raasi Bhavani.
(Whoever meditates on you, O Bhavani, receives success.)
Sakal srishti palak Durga Bhavani.
(You are the protector of the entire creation, Durga Bhavani.)
Gauri Shankar sang virajati suhavani.
(O Gauri, you look beautiful seated with Shankar.)
Mangal karan Kali mai kehlavani.
(You are called Kali Mai, the cause of all auspiciousness.)
Dhyan dharat jo koi nar Bhavani.
(Whoever meditates on you, O Bhavani.)
Sakal srishti mein hot subani.
(Their reputation spreads throughout the entire creation.)
Shri Shailputri Chalisa ka paath.
(Reciting the Shri Shailputri Chalisa.)
Karat dhyan jas aapni daas.
(Meditating on you like your own servant.)
Vinay Ram Das manu preetam Bhavani.
(Ram Das prays, O beloved Bhavani.)
Tasu dhyan se sakal srishti Bhavani.
(By meditating on you, the entire creation thrives, O Bhavani.)

faq.a1: Maa Shailputri is the form of Goddess Parvati who was born as the daughter of the Himalayas. She is the deity worshipped on the first day of Navratri.
faq.q2: What is the main benefit of worshiping her?
faq.a2: Maa Shailputri is the presiding deity of the 'Muladhara Chakra.' Worshiping her brings stability, determination, and positive energy into life.
faq.q3: What is the most auspicious day to worship her?
faq.a3: The first day of Navratri (Pratipada) is dedicated to Maa Shailputri.
faq.q4: What should be offered to Maa Shailputri?
faq.a4: Offering pure cow's ghee to Maa Shailputri is considered extremely auspicious and beneficial.
faq.q5: How is this form of the Goddess identified?
faq.a5: Maa carries a Trishul (trident) in one hand and a lotus flower in the other. She rides a Vrishabha (bull).
